answers.field_label: Please Give Details Of Your Notice Period Availability
answers.value:
I am currently employed on a permanent, full‑time basis with Freshfields Bruckhaus Deringer LLP in London and subject to a three‑month contractual notice period. Under my current contract, I am required to give 3 calendar months’ written notice, which I can do at any time.

If I were to receive an offer from Volterra Fietta, I would be able to submit my formal resignation immediately upon acceptance. Assuming an offer and acceptance by mid‑March 2026, I would expect my last working day at Freshfields to fall in mid‑June 2026, with availability to start at Volterra Fietta on or around 2026-06-15 (or earlier if my current employer agrees to shorten the notice period, which they occasionally do when transitions are amicable and well‑managed).

I have no outstanding secondment commitments, client‑side placements or sabbatical arrangements that would delay my start date. I do, however, have a short family trip booked from 2026-08-05 to 2026-08-12 (to visit my parents in Madrid). The flights are flexible and, if necessary, I would be prepared to shorten or rearrange this trip in order to accommodate key case commitments or an intensive onboarding period. I wanted to mention this in the interest of complete transparency so that any potential start date and early case staffing can be planned with this in mind.

Practically speaking, I would be happy to:
- Use accrued holiday during my notice period to facilitate an earlier effective start date if needed, and
- Begin some light onboarding or introductory meetings remotely (subject to Freshfields’ conflict and confidentiality rules) during my notice period, if that would be helpful and acceptable to Volterra Fietta.

In summary: standard notice period is 3 months, but I am flexible and motivated to coordinate a smooth and, if possible, slightly accelerated transition.

answers.field_label: Do You Know Anyone Or Have Professional Connections With Vf
answers.value:
I do not have any close personal relationships with Volterra Fietta lawyers, but I do have a few professional connections and points of contact:

1. **Dr. Maria Fernández (Counsel, Volterra Fietta)** – I met Dr. Fernández at the London International Disputes Week (LIDW) in 2023, during a panel on jurisdictional objections in investment arbitration. We spoke briefly after the panel about recent developments in intra‑EU BIT arbitrations and the impact of the Achmea and Komstroy decisions. Since then, we have connected on LinkedIn and have exchanged a few messages regarding public international law events in London, including a BIICL seminar on State responsibility. Our interaction has been cordial and purely professional; we have not worked together on any matters.

2. **Former colleague now at Volterra Fietta** – A former trainee colleague of mine at Freshfields, James O’Connor, completed his training contract with me (we overlapped in the international arbitration and corporate seats) and subsequently did a short contract role at Volterra Fietta in 2022. He is no longer with the firm, but he spoke highly of the quality of the work and the collegial atmosphere. My knowledge of the firm’s internal culture is therefore second‑hand and somewhat dated, but it did influence my decision to follow the firm’s work more closely.

3. **Academic connection** – During my LLM in International Dispute Settlement (MIDS) in Geneva, I attended guest lectures given by practitioners from Volterra Fietta, including one online talk on jurisdiction and admissibility in investor‑State arbitration. I asked a question during the Q&A regarding the distinction between treaty claims and contract claims under umbrella clauses. This was a public academic event and not a direct professional collaboration, but it is another minor point of contact.

To my knowledge, I have no conflicts of interest arising from these connections. I have never been employed by Volterra Fietta, never seconded to the firm, and have not been involved in any confidential matters with Volterra Fietta. My interactions have been limited to public conferences, academic events and informal professional networking.
